judgment.summary: Rapidly growing, highly profitable software company with strong margins and a solid balance sheet, albeit trading at a premium valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 18.1%; next earnings date is 2026-08-03.
positives: Revenue growth 84.7% YoY — exceptional top-line expansion.
Operating margin 46.2% and Profit margin 43.7% — industry-leading profitability.
Return on equity 32.6% — highly efficient capital utilization.
Free cash flow $1.75B TTM — strong cash generation supports growth.
Consensus 'buy' rating from 27 analysts with a mean target of $183.73.
negatives: Forward P/E 66.55x — valuation remains very high, pricing in significant future growth.
PEG ratio 1.95 — suggests growth may not fully justify the current multiple.
Debt/Equity 2.48 — leverage is notable, though offset by strong cash flow.
Beta 1.52 — stock exhibits higher volatility than the broader market.

news.paragraph: Jim Cramer recently described Palantir as an "incredibly good company whose stock got ahead," suggesting that while the business fundamentals are strong, the current valuation may be stretched. This commentary comes as the stock trades at a significant premium, with an upcoming earnings report on August 3rd potentially serving as a catalyst for price discovery. Traders should consider the market's perception of the valuation alongside the company's rapid growth trajectory.

headlines.title: Jim Cramer on Palantir: “Incredibly Good Company Whose Stock Got Ahead”
